title: MatmlReader
text:
MatmlReader



class ansys.materials.manager.parsers.matml.matml_reader.MatmlReader(file_path: _PATH_TYPE)

Parse a MatML (engineering data xml) file.

Fills a nested dict with all the materials and their properties.
The key of the first layer are the material names.
The conversion into a specific format/object representation is implemented separately.

The data can be accessed via matml_reader.materials

title: The rest_session_client.py module
text: The rest_session_client.py module

title: The rest_session_client.py module > Summary
text:
Summary

Classes





RestSessionClient

Client for the Granta MI material-picker REST API session lifecycle.

title: The rest_session_client.py module > Description
text:
Description

Granta MI REST API session client.

Manages the full session lifecycle used by the Granta Material Picker:

Create a session (POST).

Return the browser URL so the caller can open the material picker.

Fetch selected material data (GET – blocks until a material is selected or the session times
out, depending on server-side configuration).

Delete the session (DELETE).

Authentication is handled via an MSAL-style token response dict, identical to the pattern used
in SimpleExample.py.

title: The rest_session_client.py module > Example
text: Example

title: PyMaterials Manager
text:
PyMaterials Manager

      















PyMaterials Manager is a Python package for unifying material management across the Ansys portfolio.

PyMaterials Manager is currently a proof of concept, expect things to break.
It currently provides the following features:

It defines a Material Object that can have different material models

The supported material models can be read from a running PyMAPDL instance

The supported material models can written to a running PyMAPDL / pyFluent instance

Some supported material models can be read from a MatML XML file

Some supported material models can be written to a MatML XML file

The following material models are supported:

pyMAPDL

Simple constant material properties

Piecewise linear material properties

Polynomial material properties

Anisotropic Elasticity

pyFluent

Simple constant material properties

Ideal Gas properties

title: PyMaterials Manager > Use pre-commit
text:
Use pre-commit

The style checks take advantage of pre-commit. Developers are not forced but
encouraged to install this tool by running this command:

Every time you stage some changes and try to commit them,
pre-commit only allows them to be committed if all defined hooks succeed.

title: PyMaterials Manager > Documentation and issues
text:
Documentation and issues

For comprehensive information on PyMaterials Manager, see the latest release documentation.
On the PyMaterials Manager Issues page, you can create issues to submit questions,
report bugs, and request new features. This is the best place to post questions and code.

This class is the main entry point for the Pythonic material management interface.

classmethod MaterialManager.from_grantami(granta_mi_url: str, package_name: str | None = None, verify_ssl: bool = True, oidc_config=None) -> MaterialManager

Create a MaterialManager pre-populated from a Granta MI REST session.

Opens the Granta Material Picker in the default browser, waits for the user
to select a material, then converts the response into Material objects.

Requires the grantami extra:

Parameters

granta_mi_url

python:str

Base URL of the Granta MI instance, e.g. "https://my_granta_mi_server.com".

package_name

python:str, optional

The package name used to define which material models are exported from
Granta MI.

verify_ssl

bool

Whether to verify SSL certificates.  Defaults to True.

oidc_config

MSALOIDCConfiguration | python:None

OIDC configuration used to authenticate with the identity provider. Required if
connecting to an on-premises Granta MI deployment.

Returns

MaterialManager

A new MaterialManager instance populated with the selected materials.

Raises

ImportError

If the required Granta MI REST client dependencies are not installed.



MaterialManager.read_from_grantami(granta_mi_url: str, package_name: str | None = None, verify_ssl: bool = True, oidc_config=None) -> None

Fetch materials from Granta MI and add them to the library.

Opens the Granta Material Picker in the default browser, waits for a selection, then merges
the returned materials into this manager’s library.

Requires the grantami extra:

granta_mi_url

python:str

Base URL of the Granta MI instance, e.g. "https://my_granta_mi_server.com".

Granta MI.

verify_ssl

bool

Whether to verify SSL certificates. Defaults to True.

oidc_config

MSALOIDCConfiguration | python:None

OIDC configuration used to authenticate with the identity provider. Required if
connecting to an on-premises Granta MI deployment.

Raises

ImportError

If the required Granta MI REST client dependencies are not installed.

title: RestMaterialReader
text:
RestMaterialReader



class ansys.materials.manager.parsers.rest.rest_material_reader.RestMaterialReader(raw_data: dict)

Convert a Granta MI Material Models API JSON response into a collection of Material objects.

The dict returned by
fetch_data()
wraps the model JSON payload in an outer JSON of the form
{"value": "<json-string>", "id": <int>}``m, where the ``"value" field is JSON-encoded.
This envelope is unwrapped automatically by _iter_materials().

Parameters

raw_data

python:dict

Parsed JSON response from the …/sessions/{id}/data endpoint.

RestMaterialReader.convert_materials() -> dict[str, ansys.materials.manager._models.material.Material]

Convert the raw JSON data into Material objects.

Iterates over each material record in the response and delegates to
visit_material().

Returns

python:dict[python:str, Material]

Mapping of material name to the populated
Material object.



RestMaterialReader.visit_material(material_data: dict, material_index: int) -> ansys.materials.manager._models.material.Material

Create an individual Material.

Parameters

material_data

python:dict

A single Granta MI material model object.

material_index

python:int

1-based position of this material in the response, used only for log messages.

Returns

Material

A populated Material object.



static RestMaterialReader.visit_material_model(model_class: type, model_data: dict, model_info: ansys.materials.manager.parsers._common.ModelInfo | None = None) -> ansys.materials.manager._models._common.material_model.MaterialModel | None

Instantiate and populate a single MaterialModel.

Looks up model_class in MATERIAL_MODEL_MAP unless a model_info override
is supplied directly (used when the caller has already resolved the per-ID mapping).
If no mapping is found, a warning is emitted and None is returned.

Parameters

model_class

type

The MaterialModel subclass to instantiate.

model_data

python:dict

The model section dict from the Granta MI REST response.

model_info

ModelInfo | python:None

Optional pre-resolved ModelInfo.  When None, the mapping is
looked up from MATERIAL_MODEL_MAP.

Returns

MaterialModel | python:None

A populated model instance, or None if model_class has no registered mapping.

title: RestSessionClient
text:
RestSessionClient



class ansys.materials.manager.parsers.rest.rest_session_client.RestSessionClient(base_url: str, package_name: str | None = None, verify_ssl: bool = True, oidc_config=None)

Client for the Granta MI material-picker REST API session lifecycle.

Parameters

base_url

python:str

Base URL for the REST API, e.g. "https://cloudserver.com/is".

package_name

python:str, optional

The package name used to define which material models are exported from
Granta MI.

verify_ssl

bool

Whether to verify SSL certificates.  Defaults to True.

oidc_config

MSALOIDCConfiguration | python:None

OIDC configuration used to authenticate with the identity provider.
When None (default), the configuration is resolved automatically from base_url
using get_oidc_config_for_url().
A ValueError is raised at construction time if base_url is not a recognized
Granta MI deployment and no explicit config is supplied.

title: RestSessionClient > RestSessionClient > RestSessionClient
text:
class ansys.materials.manager.parsers.rest.rest_session_client.RestSessionClient(base_url: str, package_name: str | None = None, verify_ssl: bool = True, oidc_config=None)

Client for the Granta MI material-picker REST API session lifecycle.

Parameters

base_url

python:str

Base URL for the REST API, e.g. "https://cloudserver.com/is".

package_name

python:str, optional

The package name used to define which material models are exported from
Granta MI.

verify_ssl

bool

Whether to verify SSL certificates.  Defaults to True.

oidc_config

MSALOIDCConfiguration | python:None

OIDC configuration used to authenticate with the identity provider.
When None (default), the configuration is resolved automatically from base_url
using get_oidc_config_for_url().
A ValueError is raised at construction time if base_url is not a recognized
Granta MI deployment and no explicit config is supplied.

title: RestSessionClient > Method detail
text:
Method detail



RestSessionClient.create_session(name: str = 'PyMaterials Manager') -> None

Create a new Granta MI material-picker session.

Parameters

name

python:str, optional

Human-readable session name displayed in the material picker. Defaults to
“PyMaterials Manager”.

Raises

ValueError

If a session has already been created on this client instance.

httpx.HTTPStatusError

If the server returns a non-2xx status code.



RestSessionClient.fetch_data(timeout: float = 300.0) -> dict

Retrieve material data for the session, polling until a selection is made.

The server returns a 204 response while the user is browsing the material picker
(up to _POLL_SECONDS seconds per request). This method re-issues the GET on each
204 response until either a 200 response is received or the client-side timeout
is reached.

Parameters

timeout

python:float

Total time in seconds to wait for a material selection before raising
GrantaMIError.
Defaults to 300 seconds (5 minutes).

Returns

python:dict

The parsed JSON response from the server.

Raises

GrantaMIError

If the polling loop times out without receiving a material selection, or if
the response cannot be parsed.

httpx.HTTPStatusError

If the server returns a non-2xx, non-204 status code.

ValueError

If the session has not been initialized.



RestSessionClient.delete_session() -> None

Delete a session on the server.

Raises

httpx.HTTPStatusError

If the server returns a non-2xx status code.

ValueError

If the session has not been initialized.



RestSessionClient.__enter__() -> RestSessionClient

Enter the context manager.



RestSessionClient.__exit__(exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb) -> None

Exit the context manager, cleaning up any active session and closing the HTTP client.

title: Contribute
text:
Contribute

Overall guidance on contributing to a PyAnsys library appears in the
Contribute topic
in the PyAnsys Developer’s Guide. Ensure that you are thoroughly familiar
with this guide before attempting to contribute to PyMaterials Manager.

The following contribution information is specific to PyMaterials Manager.

title: Contribute > Clone the repository and install the package
text:
Clone the repository and install the package

PyMaterials Manager uses uv for packaging and dependency management. To clone
the repository and install the latest release in development mode, run:

title: Contribute > Post issues
text:
Post issues

Use the PyMaterials Manager Issues page to submit questions, report bugs,
and request new features. When possible, use these issue templates:

Bug report template

Feature request template

If your issue does not fit into one of these categories, create your own issue.

To reach the project support team, email pyansys.core@ansys.com.

title: Contribute > View documentation
text:
View documentation

Documentation for the latest stable release of PyMaterials Manager is hosted at
PyMaterials Manager Documentation.

title: Contribute > Adhere to code style
text:
Adhere to code style

PyMaterials Manager follows the PEP8 standard as outlined in the PyAnsys Development Guide and implements style checking using
pre-commit.

To ensure your code meets minimum code styling standards, run this code:

You can also install this as a pre-commit hook by running this code:

This way, it’s not possible for you to push code that fails the style checks:
